title: A focus on unexpected surprises in RiPP natural product biosynthesis - Thuricin CD case study
publication_type: Commentary on Zhang et al. work
objective: Analyze and highlight unexpected findings in the characterization of thuricin CD biosynthesis, emphasizing the need for detailed mechanistic investigation before engineering applications and the challenges in predicting RiPP structure-function relationships
problem_statement: RiPPs are structurally diverse, genetically encoded peptides with relaxed substrate specificity of biosynthetic enzymes, making them ideal for manipulation. However, predicting structures and functional properties from gene sequences remains difficult due to intrinsic structural dynamics of precursor peptides, structural plasticity of enzyme-peptide interactions, and dynamic nature of RiPP-enzyme binding. These challenges result in surprising discoveries that challenge existing assumptions about RiPP biosynthesis

thuricin_cd_overview.significance: First sactipeptide (not lanthipeptide) to be characterized as two-component RiPP
thuricin_cd_overview.prior_knowledge: Two-component RiPPs previously restricted to lanthipeptides (haloduracin, lichenicidin, lacticin 3147)

TrnA.name: TrnA precursor peptide
TrnA.modifications: Nested thioether rings installed by TrnC/TrnD complex

TrnB.name: TrnB precursor peptide
TrnB.modifications: Nested thioether rings installed by TrnC/TrnD complex

rSAM_enzyme_mechanism.catalytic_step_2: dAdoc triggers oxidative formation of nested thioether rings
rSAM_enzyme_mechanism.cross_link_formation: Links sulfur atoms of cysteine residues to alpha-carbons in core peptide
rSAM_enzyme_mechanism.cross_link_geometry: C-terminal cysteine residues linked to N-terminal alpha-carbons

finding_1.detail: Neither TrnC nor TrnD alone can install thioether moieties into precursor peptides
finding_1.expectation: Based on two-component lanthipeptide logic, each enzyme should modify specific precursor
finding_1.surprise: Complete modification only achieved with both TrnC and TrnD simultaneously co-incubated
finding_1.mechanism: Both enzymes can cleave SAM (typical in vitro side-reaction) but require cooperation for modification

finding_2.detail: TrnC and TrnD form a tight heterodimeric protein complex
finding_2.binding_affinity: Low micromolar affinity (typical for RiPP systems)
finding_2.binding_peptide: Complex binds to both TrnA and TrnB precursor peptides
finding_2.validation: Confirmed by pull-down experiments and microscale thermophoresis binding assays

finding_3.detail: TrnC and TrnD function asymmetrically despite being two-component system
finding_3.experimental_approach: In vitro activity assays with catalytically inactive variants
finding_3.key_result: Only [4Fe-4S]1+ cluster of TrnC required for both TrnA and TrnB modification
finding_3.trnd_role: Mutation of TrnD rSAM cluster has no effect on activity
finding_3.interpretation: TrnD plays auxiliary role, not catalytic role

finding_4.background: RREs are winged helix-turn-helix motifs for precursor peptide binding
finding_4.surprise: AF3 models suggest RREs of TrnC and TrnD interact extensively to form heterodimeric interface
finding_4.novel_role: RRE used for enzyme-enzyme dimerization, not just substrate recognition
finding_4.validation: RRE deletion results in >40-fold decrease in heterodimerization binding constant

finding_5.detail: Both TrnC and TrnD have exposed RRE 'wings' at protein surface
finding_5.finding: AF3 predicts TrnA/B leader peptides preferentially interact with TrnD RRE
finding_5.caveat: Low confidence scores in structural model

predictions: TrnD plays dominant role in binding N-terminal leader peptide
TrnC rSAM active site has greater access to C-terminal core peptide
Extensive RRE-RRE interactions at heterodimeric interface
Specific leader peptide binding to TrnD RRE
structural_modeling.validation_needed: High-resolution structural biology to confirm AF3 models

key_implications.implication_1: Functional protein-protein and protein-peptide interactions vastly different across RiPP family
key_implications.implication_2: Cannot predict from current understanding of RiPP-enzyme interactions
key_implications.implication_3: Detailed mechanistic and structural studies essential before engineering
key_implications.implication_4: RRE functions beyond precursor peptide recognition

ripp_challenges: Intrinsically disordered nature of RiPP precursor peptides
Relaxed substrate specificity of PTM enzymes
Functional versatility linked to structural dynamics
research_implications: Detailed investigation of individual systems essential
Caution when making assumptions about biosynthetic pathways
Structure-function relationships remain challenging to computationally predict
Engineering applications require deeper mechanistic understanding

two_component_ripps.thuricin_cd: First two-component sactipeptide - previously uncharacterized mechanism

key_questions: Why is heterodimeric complex formation obligatory?
How does TrnD contribute catalytically without active [4Fe-4S] cluster?
What is the mechanism of RRE-RRE interaction?
How does leader peptide preferentially bind TrnD RRE?
Are these findings specific to thuricin CD or general to other rSAM enzymes?
